paper_title: Field study at Canudos Biological Station

methods.description: Method for obtaining nests of C. analis bees using trap-nests for subsequent analysis of pollen spectrum present in the nests, specifically on the walls of the brood cells and in the feces incorporated into the cocoon.

steps.description: Nests were obtained by offering trap-nests (cardboards straws, 0.8 x 10.5cm) inserted into holes drilled through wooden boards; short lengths (1.5 x 17cm) of bamboo internodes were also used, as described by Aguiar et al. (2005). A total of 224 trap-nests were made available to the bees in two sampling sites in the CBS.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and deploy trap-nests (cardboard straws, bamboo internodes) at specified sampling sites.
inputs: Trap-nest materials (cardboard straws, bamboo internodes, wooden boards)
Sampling site locations (two sites in CBS)

steps.description: ...subsequently monitored on a monthly basis for 36 months (January/2004 to December/2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor deployed trap-nests monthly for a specified duration.
outputs: Information on trap-nests containing built nests

steps.description: Any nests built by the bees were removed to the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect built nests from trap-nests and transfer to laboratory.
outputs: Collected bee nests in the laboratory
